All right, welcome back. So let's start building some ads. So even if you've run like a million ads before Please literally follow along with what we're going to do in this training like Step by step and just check every single box I think you're gonna find this refreshingly different from what you might have tried before and I think you're gonna find that it works better and you make more money So I'm building this to be applicable and useful for longtime veterans of running ads at the same time This is also applicable and useful to people who have never run ads or who have run ads before and Haven't had any success and we're gonna start with the Facebook. All right, so What we're gonna do is I'm gonna give you a big picture overview of here's what to always do when we start a new campaign In this video and then in the next one. I'm going to demonstrate What I tell you to do in this particular video. All right, so let's go to the beautifully beautifully designed Keynote presentation here. All right, so Here's how we start a new campaign, you know, so this even if you've been doing this stuff forever We like you've already got a product. It doesn't matter This is really what I want you to do just humor me My guess here is you're gonna make more money if you do this if you do anything else And I'm basing that guess on the fact that that's generally what happens with everybody that I talked to So I'm going based on anecdotal experience, but a lot of it. So first of all, here are some things that we really have to accept It's true thing number one is that Facebook's targeting isn't necessarily real and Here's what I mean. Let's say you want to target people who are you know, you want to sell dog training material and If we wanted to do that like an obvious thing might be to start targeting people who like dogs Or targeting people who like dog training or whatever Well, just because Facebook thinks they like dogs and thinks they want like dog training doesn't necessarily mean that they really do So that's thing number one. That's like critical here and and here's the deal about that You probably set up your Facebook account ages ago, right? And you might have even put in your profile that you like dogs that you have a dog or whatever Or maybe like you clicked on an ad that had a dog in it And therefore the algorithm starting to think that you like dogs or maybe like someone else You know was on your computer one of your kids was logged into your computer or your iPad or something and Facebook was on and Your kids watching a million videos on YouTube or something with dogs in there You know all of that kind of stuff could lead Facebook's algorithm to think this person's a dog lover when in fact like you might not be So that's what I mean by Facebook's Targeting isn't necessarily real all the time and that's totally no big deal The other thing is that Facebook's audiences are essentially a moving target Our brains want us to believe that People are on Facebook 24 7 doing this right so if I we run an ad campaign And let's say we're running it let's say it's a retargeting campaign just to really put this into context And we're retargeting you know a thousand people that might have visited our site yesterday or something Our brain is gonna be like well, man I'm telling Facebook to show this ad to these one thousand people our brain is like okay So one thousand people are staring at their phone all day waiting to see our ad that that's not the case And it doesn't matter if we're showing that to an audience of a thousand people or even if we're showing it to a super broad audience Check this out. Alright, so here we are at the beautiful whiteboard the digital whiteboard and let's say you're targeting You know an audience of people that Facebook tells you one of their interests is Adwords alright, and so I have a pretty good luck with that audience by the way And Facebook's gonna tell you that's a big-ass audience, you know, and so our brain is gonna think okay Like everyone that's gonna see this ad loves Adwords first of all they genuinely like it says so right there on Facebook So we know that's not true like the Facebook will draw that conclusion based on all kinds of behavior That they can track which is getting increasingly diminished by the way so Facebook thinks they like Adwords So that's thing number one So we got this video or not this video this big audience of people who may or may not like Adwords now our brain is going to tell us They're like all of these people to say this to million people here That all of these two million people are gonna be on Facebook all day every day in reality Here's what happens, you know It's like five o'clock in the morning and like this little group of the people is on Facebook and then six o'clock in the morning rolls around This little segment of all this audience is on Facebook but these guys get off of Facebook and You know seven in the morning rolls around and this little segment of the audience is on Facebook and these people aren't on anymore and Then eight rolls around and these segments come on and these folks are still on and so forth So essentially what's happening is like various little pockets of each audience are Showing up on the platform at any given moment at any given time during the day So it's always like this moving target so you have this situation where your target audience isn't necessarily as targeted as we think and They're like coming in and out of Facebook at all times Facebook is an auction at the end of the day is generally speaking You know there's custom bidding you can do but generally speaking They're gonna show the best traffic to the people who are spending the most money and they're willing to pay the most So all this stuff is like really really fluid. So with that said all right. Hang on There we go back to the right area here with all that said it's really smart for us to embrace the fact that Facebook's AI is Really freaking smart. They're artificial intelligence their algorithm. It's it's really smart So even though like if we say okay, you know show this ad to dog lovers Even though like who knows half of the people that it thinks love dogs might not love dogs at all And even though that entire audience is you know at any given moment You never know who the hell within that audience is actually going to be on the platform Even though all those things are seemingly a big problem even though they exist the truth out of the matter is Facebook's artificial intelligence Will actually start finding you the people you want The longer you run ads and the more data that it gets in terms of okay These are the people that they want and the only way is gonna get that is if you like Actually test an experiment with a lot of stuff, right? So if we accept that to be true We can realize that the actual ad itself is really just as important as Who you're targeting? And I'll show you the logic behind that and just a minute But here's one thing that's really really important and again like even if you've been doing this stuff forever There's something you have to accept is true and that's this if we start an ad campaign today All right, and it works great in it. We start scaling it up, you know, and we triple the budget We quadruple the budget we 11x the budget or whatever eventually the campaign is gonna go lame All right, it's gonna stop working doesn't matter doesn't mean our business is broken doesn't mean the ad is broken Doesn't mean we have to we're screwed doesn't mean anything actually It just means that eventually all campaigns quit working and you have to mess with it and that's totally okay As long as you understand it in advance What happens is stuff will start working and our brains are like oh my gosh We're gonna be rich and we never have to do anything again And then when it starts performing at a level that we don't you know that we want to be better or like oh man There's a serious problem everything's horrible and that's just not the case. You just have to adjust things all the time So if we accept all of this to be true, here's the deal here's like what you need to do Number one is keep everything especially in the beginning and it doesn't matter if you've done this a million years or not Whenever you're starting a new campaign you want to keep everything super simple and you don't want to be attached So here's what I mean number one always start with a super simple ad using a super simple image and Totally obvious copy now the reason you want to do this is because of the fact that the ad itself It's really really important and the ad itself is really really important because Facebook's Artificial intelligence and their machine learning is ultimately more important than the audience selection So if your ad is blatantly obvious and says something like click this ad If you want to train your dog Right and people start clicking on it and people start buying stuff or opting in or whatever Regardless who the audience is that you're targeting Facebook or instagram or really this is this is applicable to any ad platform But we're just gonna say facebook for now The platform is going to be like okay regardless of the target that we're after This person bob here bob just bought this dog training product We analyze like 10,000 data points about bob. Let's look at our entire user database of you know 2. something billion people or however many people are in there and let's find everybody with similarities To bob and this let's look within the audience This person has specified and let's start showing the ad to more people like bob within that audience Right, so if we accept that to be how it works, which is how stuff works Then it makes total sense for our ad copy and our ad image to be like extremely obvious like Click here if you want to buy books about dog training You know because the only people are we were going to click that are people who want to buy books about dog training And therefore facebook is learning from action Okay, here are the people that this person wants right and so facebook despite what anyone wants to say facebook google youtube instagram Whatever they want you to be successful. They want your your ads to be extremely profitable Because you're going to keep advertising with them. That is their sole revenue source, right? Like that's how they make their money. Maybe they make it in some other ways. Well, like that's the main thing So there's no conspiracy. They don't want to keep you down or anything like that They want you to succeed. So step number one is Sure, we're going to get really creative and fancy with ad copy and images and videos and all that stuff But when you're starting out, we want to tell facebook Here's exactly who I want and the only way to do that is through user behavior So we want to call out to the perfect person. So let me give you an example All right, here we are in uh, we're in my facebook account right now and this is a small test that I did I actually did this as just a demonstration that worked. I left it on and I have not touched it So i'm glad that it's working. All right, so i'm making a little bit of money. That's pretty good Okay, but here I am. I got a couple of giant audiences, right? So this is literally like look at my audience It's like show this to everybody who likes social media marketing I'm only spending 20 a day on this thing and the audience size is freaking huge, right? It's everybody who likes social media marketing. Allegedly Okay, this in the united states canada, uk or australia and they're between 18 and 65 doesn't matter if they're male or female So it's a huge freaking audience of 23 million people You know, so my ad like yeah, sure I could try to captivate people with a compelling story and all this stuff and I really might do that later It might make sense, but I always start with ads that look like this which are Super basic. All right. And so the image to me is something I never obsess over I arbitrarily and randomly choose images generally the images of me and natalia and the reason I do that is because Number one, I just had good luck with them and number two I definitely know i'm not gonna get in trouble for using them, you know, like sometimes like, you know You might want to use stock images or whatever and you think it's okay to use them But I'm like weird about that kind of stuff. So I'm like, all right I want to make sure that I'm not accidentally using a copyrighted image or something in my ad So I just choose random crap off my phone and throw it up there, right? So I have zero logic at this early stage in terms of what to choose for an image. I give it no thought whatsoever You know, I mean, obviously I try not to put anything gross up there, you know Like if it's a picture of me picking my nose or something I don't think that would work as effectively as me and the Mrs. Is kind of hanging out here But uh, that's all I give thought to in terms of the images the real thing I want you to notice here about this ad Is how simple it is. All right. It is literally two sentences. This is sentence number one Get four marketing courses from frankern for four dollars. Now This is being shown to 23 million people across four countries So you might be like, oh, well, you know, these guys know who you are and you've got your name right there in the ad and stuff Man, these people don't know me from Adam. All right. My email list is like 100,000 people You know, I'm not some big famous person. He's 23 million people like of those 23 million people Maybe Maybe 100,000 of them know who I am maybe and I think that's being very very generous by the way So you're you're probably like, why in the hell would you write copy that says get four marketing courses from frankern for four dollars? Well, there's a couple reasons number one The only Reason somebody would click on this ad is if they're interested in buying marketing courses. All right, so If we understand that okay at the end of the day, it's the dang algorithms that drive all this stuff It's facebook's ad platforms their algorithm their machine learning google's the same way just like everything doesn't matter Right. It's all driven by these algorithms of this machine learning Then I want someone who's freaking interested in buying courses about marketing to click the dang ad So it'll start showing more people or it'll start showing my ads to more people like that, dude Right. So basically we could take out frankern here and just say get four marketing courses from this dude for four dollars But I figure hey some percentage of these people might google me and then they'll go to my site and they'll buy something That's why I have my name there. It's like brand building but not really the real purpose of this is to get a sale Right and if we look at the the bottom copy here just this four complete marketing courses for four dollars That's it. That's the entire ad now. Look how this ad is performed. All right. So in this particular ad set Where are we right here? Oh, let me get out of there. All right, so Um, let me get back over here get back over here. There we go. This hasn't been running that long But I've made money on it. You know, I'm showing it to these giant freaking audiences. Look at the digital marketing one Okay, so the digital marketing one is 31 million people I can bet you a dollar 52 and I don't have that kind of money laying around but I know where to get it Okay, I know people so I could come up with it if I had to but I'd be willing to bet a dollar 52 That not all 31 million that these people know me like most of them don't nor do they give it a single dam about me You know, it doesn't matter So I'm using the picture of me because I own it right and I'm putting my name in there because it's my stuff And it's like well, they might as well get used to seeing my name. Who knows, you know, you show your name a lot It's a nice byproduct of having the ad but the real thing about this ad Is that I'm deliberately utilizing their Algorithm by basically having copied this as click this if you want to buy marketing courses I mean, that's pretty much what it says, right? So we'll create a new ad here In a minute, but I wanted to show you the logic there because one of the things I deal with with veterans and with new people And it doesn't matter is people will obsess over the ad and it's worth obsessing over but not at first At first we want to train that pixel and be like, hey, this is who I'm looking for Okay, and then it'll start going out and it'll know to show your stuff to people like that and then we can start Getting fancy. Okay. All right. So the next thing you want to do is when once you have the ad made That's like really obvious and borderline dumb is I like to use this two pronged test approach Right. So I I build two campaigns. I'm just going to kind of diagram it out for you Then we're going to build it here in the next video I build two campaigns and the first one is I take a bunch of different audiences And I put them in a campaign and I assign a small budget to each audience Right. So I'll show you how facebook works in a second and you probably already know so I'll make it really quick But let me show you the second campaign test. I try to run concurrently. All right, so that's one test The second one is I take a small handful of wide open super broad audience Audiences with like basically no targeting and I have slightly bigger daily budgets for those and I run that campaign as well and essentially and I'll diagram this for you in a minute essentially This like is always the jumping off point and it doesn't matter, you know, if I'm starting some if I'm selling something I've never sold before or I just want to test a new ad or whatever. This is always the way I do it This leads to great stuff. It's the least amount of risk and A key key metric for you here is always go into these tests knowing what you're willing to pay for a customer All right, so I know I'm willing to pay like really like, you know $27 or something to get a customer for my four dollar thing because if you remember that video I showed you they're worth in 30 days They're worth like 31 You know, so I'm willing to like pay a premium for them because hey I know I'm gonna make money in the first 30 days and the second 30 days. I'll probably make more All right, so I know that so know what you're willing to pay for a customer So you're not just sort of blindly throw in stuff at the wall and also expect most of these audiences to fail All right, so if we test, you know, usually I'll test like 20 different audiences at $5 a day And I'll choose like, you know stuff like people who like digital marketing people who like social media people who like gary vader Grant others like Arbitrarily come up with audiences because I don't freaking know neither does facebook really, you know, they're guessing All right, so I'll test like 20 of them. All right, and I'll run them at five bucks a day And I know that if I'm testing 20 audiences 18 of those audiences aren't gonna work but two will Okay, and the final thing is before I diagram this out for you is you always want to run them until you spent the amount You're willing to pay per customer in each ad set So if I'm willing to spend 26 to get a customer Then I'm going to run this campaign. Let's say I'm spending, you know, five dollars per day per audience I'm going to run this thing for about five days like five days and five and a half days So basically as soon as I've spent 26 dollars in each ad set Then I'm going to evaluate it, right? And the reason why is because you know, I want to give each ad set each audience I want to give it the ability to get a customer, right? Because that's all we're trying to do right now is to see, all right, you know Where are the customers? Okay, and also train facebook to look for who I want to look for so let me draw this out for you on this incredibly nice Beautiful and professionally done whiteboard here. All right. Now. This is really Picasso level stuff. I know so let me get that out of the way so As a refresher or an introduction if you've never run facebook ads the structure of it's like this So we're going to take one campaign All right, this is going to be campaign number one And we'll call this one Bat Which stands for buncha? Audiences test. All right, so this is the campaign and basically this is the place where we're saying, okay, facebook We want to start a campaign and we want you to get this video music We want you to get as conversions or we want you to get us engagement or whatever. That's where you tell facebook You know what your objective is so this is going to be a conversion campaign always is to start All right, and so and don't worry. I'm going to build one with you in a second. I'm just drawing this out for you All right, and so within that campaign I'm going to Create one ad set and the ad set is simply this this is where you're telling facebook who to show your ad to Right, so i'm going to show my ad to people in the united states between 18 and 65 who like marketing or something So that's going to be my first audience. All right, so that's all that really happens in an ad set All right, so this is audience number one and then i'm going to show them that super simple ad All right, and then i'm going to take this this is my ad All right, and then i'm going to take this and i'm just going to duplicate it But audience number two is going to be a different group of people. They're going to see the same ad All right, and just going to put a there so it won't take forever audience number three These guys are going to see the same ad all right And i'm going to do that as many times as i can so i like to do a test like this You know where i'm spending let's say five dollars a day For each one of these audiences and i like to put 20 audiences in one of these suckers And the reason i like to start small is because i think and this is just from my experience I don't have any documentation like you know I don't have any studies that have been done by harvard or anything to back this up But based on what i've learned over the past couple of years from doing this It seems like facebook's algorithm works harder When you have a very low budget at first for each individual ad set so this is campaign number one now campaign number two all right c2 Same thing this is going to be a conversion campaign So that's all you're doing as you just say on facebook. I want you to go out and get me conversions That's basically what you do when you say it's create a campaign. All right, and i'm going to call this one b a That's things for big-ass audiences, right and in this one. I'm not going to have as many audiences and i'm going to have like all us Right so literally everyone in the united states over 18 and then i might have Everyone in australia over 18 And then i might have everyone in the uk Over 18. All right, so like super broad audiences and then i might have everyone in canada over the age of 18 All right, and depending on like your comfort level And incidentally i'm going to show them all the exact same ad all right that's super simple ad So they're all seeing the same thing out. So i won't draw it out because it'll be boring All right, and this one depending on comfort level. I might say all right screw it You know i want to get some data really quickly I'm going to spend 20 bucks a day for each one of these because there's not as many Or i might be like really conservative and be like no I'm just going to spend five dollars a day and see what happens right and so you might be like dude That's crazy targeting every single person in the u.s. And sometimes i'll literally target every single person in the world Like literally everyone worldwide that's over the age of 18 and speaks english um and yeah, I do run Campaigns with that giant of an audience and a lot of times it works great because of the freaking algorithm Right, so they're machine learning is so smart That it'll be like all right I'm just gonna go find people like the ones that are already buying your stuff and i'm going to show ads to them And the advantage to doing this is that it's usually and sometimes not but generally speaking it's cheaper Right because the fewer targeting restrictions you put on a campaign The the less expensive it is to show that audience your ads. So it's kind of cool, right? So this is the big picture and let's say You know each one of these is five dollars a day and so i'm setting it up soon You know facebook spent five dollars a day on each one of these adsets You know and so i maybe we have 20 of them in this one and maybe we have like five of them in this one Yeah, so we got five in this one So that would be 125 dollars a day for spending five dollars on each of these I want to spend in total per ad set. I'm going to spend like 25 bucks I'm going to run it for five days and my mission isn't necessarily to make money If I do you know because I have upsells and stuff that's great But what i'm really looking for is to find audiences that'll give me customers Within the price range that i'm willing to pay Because once we have that data we can do all kinds of stuff We can use all these cool advanced bidding things that I showed you and like really really go nuts You know we can do scaling we can increase the budget as we can duplicate there's a gazillion things we can do So doing it this way really eliminates your risk as opposed to what most people want to do Which is i'm going to find the perfect audience. I'm going to write the perfect ad I'm going to put all my eggs in that one basket pretty much guaranteed failure and if like you know Two of these audiences work. That's fine. You know, we haven't wasted a lot of money We haven't lost a lot of money nothing bad has really happened And we found two audiences that work and we can build on stuff and three of them work That's fine and none of them work. That's okay because we haven't risked a ton of money, right? So that's the guiding principle of this very first phase as we start with the super simple image By the way, we're using an image at first instead of a video because it's cheaper And it's easier to start it's faster. So when you're running a video ad, it's generally more expensive than an image ad Right, so that's why we're starting with an image. It's faster. It's cheaper We're just looking to see what their algorithm is going to turn out this all we're doing here. All right So this is phase number one and in the next video. We're going to actually build a starter campaign Okay, so i'll see you then
